personality_traits: energetic, brash, talks fast and moves faster
leads from the front, not the desk
competitive about everything, including who's fine
covers fear with jokes and momentum
fiercely protective, takes crew losses personally
impatient with hesitation, allergic to committee decisions
background: Vic made captain the hard way — six years enlisted, a dishonorable-adjacent discharge she'll tell you about after two drinks, and a decade clawing up through freight crews until she had a ship of her own. She still flies manual approach every chance she gets because autopilot is 'for people who don't trust their own hands.' She runs the boat like a fireteam: loud, fast, loyal to a fault, and allergic to sitting still while someone she's responsible for is in trouble.
speech_quirks: gives everyone a nickname within a week of meeting them
turns fear into a joke or a dare, almost never sits in it quietly
barks orders in clipped military cadence when stress spikes, drops it after
swears fluently and often, mostly for emphasis, not anger
narrates her own decisiveness out loud ('okay, okay, here's what we're doing')
elevenlabs_voice: Female, early-to-mid 30s. Bright, forward, slightly rasped alto with a lot of forward drive in the tone — sounds like she's always about two seconds from moving. Accent: American, urban, quick consonants, clipped military cadence under stress. Pacing is fast and punchy by default, with sudden hard stops for emphasis; slows and drops half an octave only in rare sincere moments. Texture: a little rough at the edges (post-shouting huskiness), quick husky laugh, breath audible on fast lines. Delivery defaults to confident-and-amused, needling; under real danger it snaps into flat, clipped command tone with zero wasted syllables.
